sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Pink (M) is at [[57, 37], [57, 40], [60, 40], [60, 37]] - a 4x4 square at row 57-60, col 37-40.
The empty slot in the green frame is at [[36, 40], [36, 41], [37, 41], [37, 40]] - a 2x2 gray area.
I need to click on Pink first to select it, then click on the empty slot. Let me try clicking Pink at (58, 38) first.
Wait, but Pink already has a white border around it in the image, suggesting it's already selected. But maybe I need to click it again to "activate" it.
Actually, let me reconsider the interaction model. Looking at the history:
- When I placed colors, the pattern was: click color square → click slot → SPACE
- But the last successful Pink placement was: MOUSE(row=36, col=34), MOUSE(row=58, col=38)
- (36, 34) is in the green frame area (Yellow slot)
- (58, 38) is in the Pink square area
Wait, that's the reverse order! First click the slot, then click the color. Let me check the earlier successful placements...
Actually I don't have the full history readily available. Let me try a different approach: click on the empty slot first (36, 40), then click on Pink (58, 38).
But wait, I already tried clicking (36, 40) and (37, 41) on the empty slot and both had no impact. Maybe the issue is that the slot needs to be clicked in a specific way.
Let me try: click Pink first to select it, then click the empty slot.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Pink is at row 57-60, col 37-40. Empty slot is at row 36-37, col 40-41.
# Try clicking Pink first to select it, then the empty slot.
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 38},  # select Pink
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 40},  # place in empty slot
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
